_schema.purpose: Why this term exists in Pinokio and what user-facing concept it represents.
_schema.context: Where the term appears in the app and what surrounding workflow it belongs to.
_schema.translationRule: How to translate it, including what to keep literal and what behavior the wording must not imply.
_schema.translate: Whether the visible term should normally be localized.
_schema.examples: Representative English source strings that should follow this entry.

_policy.sourceOfTruth: Translate from the English catalog using this glossary before writing non-English catalogs.
_policy.qualityContract: Every translation must also satisfy QUALITY.md. Glossary nuance is necessary but not sufficient; raw keys, English carryover, mixed-language token output, literal preposition translation, bad fragment sources, and English-order sentence assembly are blocking failures.
_policy.promotion: A locale present in catalogs.json is not automatically production-ready. It may be enabled only after the strict localization audit and visual QA pass.
_policy.scope: Visible Pinokio product UI only. Do not translate third-party app metadata, paths, config keys, terminal output, exported diagnostic/report payload bodies, or values that app/plugin logic may read.
_policy.placeholders: Preserve placeholders exactly, including braces and names such as {name}, {count}, {path}, and {message}.
_policy.literals: Preserve product names, file names, URLs, code identifiers, and tool names listed as translate:false or preserve entries.
_policy.style: Use natural concise app UI language, not literal sentence-by-sentence translation. Commands should stay short and action-oriented.

Pinokio.purpose: Protect the product identity across all app chrome, settings, agent copy, and file-location copy.
Pinokio.context: Brand/product references, Pinokio-managed folders, Pinokio Network, prompts that mention the app itself.
Pinokio.translationRule: Keep exactly "Pinokio" in every locale. Translate surrounding words only.
Pinokio.meaning: The product name. Keep it as Pinokio in every locale.

app.purpose: Name the local installable/runnable unit Pinokio users manage from Home and app pages.
app.context: Home list, app actions, autolaunch, app search, file browser, and settings copy.
app.translationRule: Translate as an application/project in Pinokio, not specifically a phone app or web page.
app.meaning: A local Pinokio-installed project or launcher under the user's Pinokio api folder, not specifically a mobile app.

plugin.purpose: Name an extension that adds actions, editors, terminal agents, or other capabilities to Pinokio.
plugin.context: Plugins list, plugin detail pages, install plugin flow, desktop/terminal plugin categories.
plugin.translationRule: Translate as extension/plugin for Pinokio. Do not imply browser plugin unless the locale normally uses that borrowed term generically.
plugin.meaning: A Pinokio extension/tool that adds capabilities to Pinokio. It is not a browser plugin.

launcher.purpose: Describe the script/config layer that installs, runs, opens, or customizes a Pinokio app.
launcher.context: Home action menus, editor warnings, create/edit launcher actions, install/run script choices.
launcher.translationRule: Use a practical term for runner/launch script. Avoid marketing/event launch meanings.
launcher.meaning: A Pinokio script or configuration that installs, runs, or opens an app.
launcher.nuance: Prefer a practical UI term meaning runner/launch script, not a marketing launch.

home.purpose: Refer to Pinokio storage roots or the top-level Pinokio app screen depending on the source string.
home.context: Settings home folder, main navigation, empty Home screen, storage path warnings.
home.translationRule: When paired with folder/path, translate as storage/home directory. Do not translate as browser homepage.
home.meaning: Usually the Pinokio home folder where Pinokio stores apps, cache, bin, drive, and environment data. Do not translate as a web browser home page when the context is a storage path.
examples: Set and save a Pinokio home folder

This machine.purpose: Distinguish the current computer from local-network Pinokio instances.
This machine.context: Main sidebar computer section and local-device navigation.
This machine.translationRule: Translate as this/current computer. Do not imply server, website, or remote device.
This machine.meaning: The current computer running this Pinokio instance.

Local network.purpose: Group Pinokio instances discoverable on the same LAN.
Local network.context: Main sidebar computer section and network sharing/discovery UI.
Local network.translationRule: Translate as local network/LAN. Do not imply public internet.
Local network.meaning: Other Pinokio instances discoverable on the user's local LAN.

Open without launching.purpose: Preserve a non-starting path into an app view.
Open without launching.context: Home app action modal and run-mode navigation when a user wants to inspect without running scripts.
Open without launching.translationRule: Make clear that the app opens/navigates but no script/process starts. Keep distinct from Run and Launch.
Open without launching.meaning: Open the app view/folder without automatically running its launch or start command.

terminal.purpose: Refer to Pinokio-managed shell/process sessions, including agent sessions.
terminal.context: Terminal view, terminal sessions UI, logs, shell environment inspection, upload/download status.
terminal.translationRule: Translate as terminal/shell session. Do not use website login session language.

mode.purpose: Expose a runtime or launch behavior choice while preserving internal option values.
mode.context: Settings mode selector, terminal agent mode selector, run-mode copy.
mode.translationRule: Translate visible labels only. Keep stored config identifiers such as desktop/background unchanged.
mode.meaning: The Pinokio app runtime mode, currently desktop or background. Keep option values in config as English identifiers; translate only visible labels.

Auto.purpose: Let Pinokio choose behavior from browser/system defaults.
Auto.context: Language settings and other automatic preference choices.
Auto.translationRule: Translate as automatic/system-following behavior. In locale settings it means Accept-Language first, then English only when no supported locale matches.
Auto.meaning: Use browser/system preference detection. In locale settings, Auto means Accept-Language first; English is the default locale only when no supported locale matches.

Reset.purpose: Warn that local Pinokio-managed state will be discarded or recreated.
Reset.context: Settings troubleshoot buttons, Git/file change discard flows, app reset confirmations.
Reset.translationRule: Use a short destructive command. It should feel stronger than refresh/reload.
Reset.meaning: Delete/recreate local Pinokio-managed state for a specific subsystem. It is potentially destructive.

ENVIRONMENT.purpose: Refer to the literal Pinokio environment file.
ENVIRONMENT.context: Settings advanced environment editor, reset environment variables copy.
ENVIRONMENT.translationRule: Keep uppercase ENVIRONMENT when referring to the file name. Translate surrounding copy only.
ENVIRONMENT.meaning: The literal Pinokio environment file name. Keep uppercase when referring to the file.

SKILL.md.purpose: Refer to the literal Markdown instruction file consumed by Pinokio agents.
SKILL.md.context: Settings docs download actions and agent/skill documentation references.
SKILL.md.translationRule: Keep SKILL.md literal. Translate words like download/guide around it.
SKILL.md.meaning: A literal Markdown file name used by Pinokio agents. Do not translate.

Run.context: Home run buttons, terminal start controls, app run tabs, install/run script flows.
Run.translationRule: Translate as execute/run. Do not weaken it into open/navigate.
Run.meaning: Execute a Pinokio script or start an app process. This is a command verb, not a general navigation/open action.

Open.purpose: Navigate to an existing page, file, folder, browser URL, or plugin surface.
Open.context: Open in browser/file explorer, open project, open publish, open plugin, navigation controls.
Open.translationRule: Translate as open/navigate. Do not imply starting a script unless the English source says Run or Launch.
Open.meaning: Navigate to a view, file, folder, browser page, or plugin surface. Do not imply starting a process unless the English source says Run or Launch.

Launch.purpose: Start an external app/plugin or begin a launch flow.
Launch.context: Plugin detail actions, desktop plugin buttons, terminal plugin launch copy.
Launch.translationRule: Translate as launch/start, with external-vs-inside Pinokio distinction preserved by surrounding copy.
Launch.meaning: Start an external desktop app or begin a plugin/app launch flow. In plugin context, desktop plugins launch externally while terminal plugins launch inside Pinokio.

Install.purpose: Set up apps, plugins, tools, bundles, or dependencies inside Pinokio-managed storage.
Install.context: Explore, plugins, tools, checkpoints, app prerequisites, fresh install guidance.
Install.translationRule: Translate as install/setup locally. Do not imply OS-wide installation unless source mentions package/dependency.
Install.meaning: Download, clone, or set up dependencies/plugins/apps into local Pinokio-managed storage. It is not an OS-wide installation unless the UI explicitly says dependency package.

Update.purpose: Apply a newer version or pull latest code.
Update.context: Home update script toast, plugin update actions, file update commands.
Update.translationRule: Translate as update/bring current. Keep distinct from refresh/reload status.
Update.meaning: Fetch or apply the latest version of an app/plugin/script. Do not translate as a generic status refresh.

Refresh.purpose: Reload current UI data or status without changing installed code.
Refresh.context: List refresh, log tree refresh, status checks, shell environment reloads.
Refresh.translationRule: Translate as refresh/reload. Do not imply installing an update.
Refresh.meaning: Reload status, lists, trees, or current view data without changing installed code.

checkpoint.purpose: Name a restorable saved dependency/workspace state.
checkpoint.context: Checkpoints page, app snapshot UI, delete/install checkpoint actions.
checkpoint.translationRule: Translate as checkpoint/saved state/version point, not as a checklist item.
checkpoint.meaning: A saved local workspace/app state snapshot used for restore/version history. Do not translate as a generic checklist item.

snapshot.purpose: Name one saved point-in-time restore object.
snapshot.context: Checkpoint history, snapshot JSON metadata, save checkpoint modal.
snapshot.translationRule: Translate as snapshot/version snapshot. Do not translate as screenshot.
snapshot.meaning: A saved checkpoint instance at a point in time. In Checkpoints UI it is a restoreable version, not a screenshot.

local changes.purpose: Describe uncommitted local Git/workspace/plugin file changes.
local changes.context: App Changes panel, plugin detail status, terminal workspace Git status, publish flow.
local changes.translationRule: Translate as local file changes. Do not imply app settings or network changes.
local changes.meaning: File changes in a local Git/workspace/plugin folder that have not been saved/committed or published.

Save version.purpose: Create a local tracked version/checkpoint before publishing.
Save version.context: Plugin GitHub flow, changes panels, commit/version controls.
Save version.translationRule: Translate as save/create local version. Keep distinct from generic Save and Publish.
Save version.meaning: Create a local Git commit/version checkpoint for plugin or workspace changes.

Publish.purpose: Push or expose a saved local version through GitHub/remote repository.
Publish.context: Plugin detail GitHub flow, app publish panels, remote repository setup.
Publish.translationRule: Translate as publish/share to remote. Do not translate as local save.
Publish.meaning: Push or expose a saved plugin/workspace version through GitHub. It is not the same as saving locally.

GitHub flow.purpose: Describe the guided GitHub setup/save/publish sequence.
GitHub flow.context: Plugin detail and task share progress cards and overlays for connecting, saving, creating repositories, and publishing.
GitHub flow.translationRule: Keep GitHub literal. Translate flow/process naturally.
GitHub flow.meaning: The guided sequence for connecting, creating, saving, and publishing a plugin or task repository on GitHub.

task.purpose: Name a reusable Pinokio prompt template that can collect inputs and run through a selected tool.
task.context: Tasks library, task builder, task launch page, task install page, and task sharing flow.
task.translationRule: Translate as reusable task/action/template, not as an OS process, background job, or checklist item.
task.meaning: A Pinokio-managed reusable prompt workflow stored under the tasks folder. It is not a terminal process or TODO checklist.

prompt template.purpose: Describe the reusable task text with placeholders that become inputs.
prompt template.context: Task builder metadata and template editor.
prompt template.translationRule: Translate prompt naturally if the locale has a common AI-product term. Preserve literal placeholders such as {{repo_url}} and {{variable}} exactly.
prompt template.meaning: The reusable instruction text for an AI/tool task. Placeholder variables in double braces become structured inputs.

input.purpose: Name a structured value the user fills before running a task.
input.context: Task builder detected inputs and task launch forms.
input.translationRule: Translate as form/input value. Do not translate as terminal stdin or model context unless the source string explicitly says prompt/context.
input.meaning: A field generated from a task template placeholder and collected before running the task.

tool.purpose: Name the Pinokio plugin or runner selected to execute a task.
tool.context: Task launch tool picker, task install launch context, and task builder runtime copy.
tool.translationRule: Translate as tool/runner. Do not imply OS utility only; it may be an AI coding plugin, CLI, or desktop app.
tool.meaning: The selected Pinokio execution surface for a task, such as a CLI plugin or desktop app.

permalink.purpose: Name the stable task URL that captures task identity and input values.
permalink.context: Task launch/share link controls and copy feedback.
permalink.translationRule: Translate as permanent/share link. Make clear it is a URL, not a local file path. Preserve distinction from GitHub remote repository URL.
permalink.meaning: A URL that reopens a task with its current input values. It may be local until the task is published to GitHub.

share link.purpose: Describe a task URL meant to be copied or opened by another user or browser context.
share link.context: Task share page and task launch share controls.
share link.translationRule: Translate as shareable link. If the copy mentions GitHub, keep GitHub literal and do not imply LAN sharing.
share link.meaning: A URL for opening the task. A real cross-machine share link requires the task to be published to GitHub.

Remote ref.purpose: Show the canonical Git/GitHub task identity used to install or reopen a shared task.
Remote ref.context: Task install page before a remote task is installed locally.
Remote ref.translationRule: Keep ref short or use the locale's Git reference wording. Do not expand it into a vague reference note.
Remote ref.meaning: The normalized remote repository reference for a task package.

bookmarklet.purpose: Name the small bookmark JavaScript that sends the current browser page to Pinokio.
bookmarklet.context: Bookmarklet installation page and manual copy instructions.
bookmarklet.translationRule: Use the locale's common borrowed term if available. Keep the nuance of a bookmark-bar action, not a browser extension.
bookmarklet.meaning: A JavaScript bookmark users drag to the browser bookmarks bar. It is not a browser extension/plugin.

Connected services.purpose: Group external accounts Pinokio can authenticate with and use.
Connected services.context: Connect index and provider-specific OAuth pages.
Connected services.translationRule: Translate as connected external services/accounts. Do not imply local network devices or Pinokio peers.
Connected services.meaning: External providers such as GitHub, Hugging Face, or X.com that Pinokio can connect to through OAuth.

URL dropdown.purpose: Name the first-party URL/app/process picker attached to the app chrome address bar.
URL dropdown.context: Runtime-generated app/process dropdown, mobile Open URL modal, split view picker, and quick-create launcher modal.
URL dropdown.translationRule: Translate visible actions and empty states. Do not translate protocol names, hostnames, localhost examples, URL values, or app/process names.
URL dropdown.meaning: The address-bar picker that lets users open local URLs, Pinokio apps, running processes, or create a launcher from a prompt.

Protection.purpose: Name Pinokio’s safety gate for AI folder access.
Protection.context: Editor beta controls, terminal protection state, app run tab, permission prompts.
Protection.translationRule: Translate as protection/safeguard. Do not imply antivirus, firewall, or password protection.
Protection.meaning: A Pinokio safety feature that blocks AI agents from accessing folders outside the allowed workspace unless the user grants permission.

AI permissions.purpose: Name saved access decisions for AI agents.
AI permissions.context: Plugin detail AI permission actions, terminal permission manager, protection prompts.
AI permissions.translationRule: Translate as AI access permissions/authorizations. Do not imply model capabilities.
AI permissions.meaning: Saved folder/network access decisions for AI agents. Translate as permissions/authorization, not as model capability.

terminal session.purpose: Name a running or resumable shell/agent process column.
terminal session.context: Terminal page session columns, close session prompts, upload shell emit status.
terminal session.translationRule: Translate as terminal/shell session. Avoid login/account session wording.
terminal session.meaning: A running or resumable Pinokio-managed shell/agent session. Translate as a terminal/shell session, not a website login session.

workspace.purpose: Name a local project folder used by agents, Git, files, and deployment.
workspace.context: Terminal workspaces page, app changes view, deployment to api folder, repository panels.
workspace.translationRule: Translate as project workspace/local workspace. Do not imply cloud office workspace.
workspace.meaning: A local project folder used by terminal agents, Git changes, file browser, and app/plugin work. It is not a cloud office workspace.

agent.purpose: Name an AI coding/automation actor launched from Pinokio.
agent.context: Terminal sessions, ask AI panels, agent chat controls, permission/protection copy.
agent.translationRule: Translate as AI agent/assistant. Do not imply a human customer-service agent.
agent.meaning: An AI coding/automation agent started from the terminal sessions UI. Keep the meaning close to AI assistant/agent, not human representative.

Guarded.purpose: Name the safer agent launch mode with default approval/sandbox controls.
Guarded.context: Terminal agent mode selector and explanatory mode metadata.
Guarded.translationRule: Translate as guarded/protected/controlled mode. Keep distinct from YOLO.
Guarded.meaning: Agent launch mode that keeps provider default approvals/sandbox controls. Translate as protected/controlled mode.

YOLO.purpose: Name the faster unsafe agent mode without approval prompts.
YOLO.context: Terminal agent mode selector and explanatory mode metadata.
YOLO.translationRule: Keep YOLO as the visible mode name. Translate explanatory copy only.
YOLO.meaning: Agent launch mode that runs without approval prompts. Keep YOLO as the visible mode name, but translate explanatory copy.

File Explorer.purpose: Name file browsing surfaces and OS file manager actions.
File Explorer.context: App sidebar, Home menu, file browser, Open in File Explorer actions.
File Explorer.translationRule: Translate as file explorer/file manager. Preserve distinction between Pinokio Files and OS File Explorer.
File Explorer.meaning: Pinokio's in-app file browser or the OS file manager depending on context. Preserve the distinction between Open in File Explorer and Open files.

logs.purpose: Name runtime diagnostic output used for troubleshooting.
logs.context: Logs page, terminal logs, app logs, report generation, privacy filter review.
logs.translationRule: Translate as logs/diagnostic records. Avoid mathematical logarithm meanings.
logs.meaning: Application/runtime diagnostic logs. Translate as logs/journals used for troubleshooting, not mathematical logarithms.

Privacy filter.purpose: Name the log review tool that masks sensitive data before sharing.
Privacy filter.context: Logs page report preview, redaction review, community support draft preparation.
Privacy filter.translationRule: Translate as privacy/sensitive-data filter. Do not imply browser privacy settings.
Privacy filter.meaning: The log review tool that masks private data before sharing a report.

redaction.purpose: Describe masking/removing sensitive text from logs.
redaction.context: Logs privacy filter counts, redaction review filters, copy-before-sharing workflow.
redaction.translationRule: Translate as masking/redaction of sensitive content, not censorship/editing style.
redaction.meaning: Masking/removing sensitive text from logs before copying or asking the community.

Ask Community.purpose: Start a support/discussion flow with the Pinokio community site.
Ask Community.context: App panels and logs report flow where a draft question opens on pinokio.co.
Ask Community.translationRule: Translate as ask the community/support community. Do not imply AI community or LAN community.
Ask Community.meaning: Open or create a support/discussion draft on the Pinokio community site.

Ask AI.purpose: Start an integrated AI helper flow with the current project/error context.
Ask AI.context: App sidebar/panels, logs help flow, terminal agent picker.
Ask AI.translationRule: Translate as ask AI/AI assistant. Preserve AI literal if common in the locale.
Ask AI.meaning: Ask the integrated AI assistant for help with the current app/error/context.

Git URL.purpose: Name the repository address a user pastes to clone, install, import, or share a Pinokio app, task, skill, or plugin.
Git URL.context: Create/import launcher forms, plugin/task/skill download forms, GitHub publish flows, and validation messages.
Git URL.translationRule: Keep Git literal. Translate URL only if the locale normally localizes the acronym in UI; never translate the actual URL value or placeholder example.
Git URL.meaning: A Git repository URL, usually from GitHub, used as an input value. It is not a general website URL unless the source explicitly says browser URL.

repository.purpose: Refer to a Git/GitHub code repository that stores a task, plugin, app, or launcher source.
repository.context: Publish/share flows, remote task install pages, prototype/import forms, and GitHub status cards.
repository.translationRule: Translate as code repository. Do not use a generic storage/archive term that hides the Git/GitHub behavior.
repository.meaning: A remote or local Git repository containing code or a Pinokio package.

starred app.purpose: Mark apps the user wants Pinokio and AI agents to prioritize.
starred app.context: Home app list star button, star/unstar tooltip text, and toast explaining AI search priority.
starred app.translationRule: Translate as favorite/starred app while preserving the priority signal. Do not imply rating/review stars.
starred app.meaning: A user preference flag that gives an app higher priority in Pinokio UI and AI tool search.

Pinokio Network.purpose: Name Pinokio’s local peer discovery, HTTPS routing, and app endpoint sharing surface.
Pinokio Network.context: Network settings, local network pages, peer access QR codes, route tables, and tab popovers.
Pinokio Network.translationRule: Keep Pinokio literal. Translate Network as the product feature area, not as generic internet or social network.
Pinokio Network.meaning: The Pinokio feature for discovering peers and exposing local endpoints across trusted local machines.

Local Share.purpose: Name the local-network QR/link flow that lets another device on the same LAN open this Pinokio instance.
Local Share.context: Network and file explorer share modals, QR codes, local proxy links, and local access-point controls.
Local Share.translationRule: Translate as local/LAN sharing or access. Do not imply public internet sharing, social sharing, or publishing a project.
Local Share.meaning: A local-network access link for this Pinokio instance. It is limited to trusted devices on the user's local network.
examples: Access Pinokio from any local network device

Public Share.purpose: Name the Cloudflare Tunnel flow that exposes a Pinokio endpoint outside the local network.
Public Share.context: Share modals and tunnel status controls that show public access links and QR codes.
Public Share.translationRule: Translate as public/external sharing or access. Preserve Cloudflare Tunnel as a product name. Do not imply GitHub publishing.
Public Share.meaning: A public tunnel URL for reaching Pinokio from outside the local network.

Peer.purpose: Identify another Pinokio instance or machine participating in local peer-to-peer access.
Peer.context: Network pages, route labels, URL dropdown host labels, and peer QR/access points.
Peer.translationRule: Translate as peer/local peer machine. Do not translate as friend, customer, or arbitrary network node.
Peer.meaning: Another Pinokio machine discovered on the local network and allowed to access peer routes.

Instant HTTPS.purpose: Name the automatic local HTTPS endpoint feature for Pinokio app routes.
Instant HTTPS.context: Network settings, app endpoint explanations, browser route lists, and HTTPS setup guidance.
Instant HTTPS.translationRule: Keep HTTPS literal. Translate Instant as automatic/immediate setup, not as speed/performance claim.
Instant HTTPS.meaning: Pinokio-provided HTTPS routes for local apps without manual certificate setup.

browser route.purpose: Name a route or URL that opens a running local app/process in the embedded browser or external browser.
browser route.context: Tab link popover, network route tables, URL dropdown, open-in-browser buttons, and app runtime chrome.
browser route.translationRule: Translate route as URL/path/route depending on locale. Preserve protocol, hostnames, ports, and concrete URL values.
browser route.meaning: A browser-addressable endpoint for a local or peer app/process.

notification sound.purpose: Name the sound preference used when a background tab or process sends a desktop/app notification.
notification sound.context: Tab notification popover, sound picker, silent/default choices, and notification flash backup display text.
notification sound.translationRule: Translate as notification alert sound. Keep sound choice names concise. Do not translate dynamic custom sound filenames.
notification sound.meaning: A UI preference for which sound Pinokio plays for notifications. It applies across tabs unless copy says otherwise.

download destination.purpose: Describe where Pinokio will clone, import, or save an app/plugin/task folder.
download destination.context: Download/install modals, inline install status, destination validation errors, and downloaded-to toasts.
download destination.translationRule: Translate as local save/download/clone destination. Preserve placeholders such as {path} and literal paths like ~/api or ~/tasks.
download destination.meaning: The local folder path inside Pinokio-managed storage that receives a downloaded or cloned repository.

skill.purpose: Name reusable instructions or capability packs Pinokio agents can use.
skill.context: Skills page, skill download/install flow, managed skills list, and SKILL.md references.
skill.translationRule: Translate as AI/agent skill or capability. Do not translate as human talent or school skill when that would sound personal.
skill.meaning: A reusable Pinokio agent capability, often backed by a SKILL.md file.

managed skill.purpose: Distinguish skills installed and controlled through Pinokio from arbitrary local files.
managed skill.context: Skills page sections, enable/disable switches, sync folder names, and installed skill cards.
managed skill.translationRule: Translate as Pinokio-managed skill/capability. Keep the administrative nuance: Pinokio can enable, disable, update, or sync it.
managed skill.meaning: A skill installation managed by Pinokio, not merely a file visible on disk.

screen capture.purpose: Name screenshots and recordings captured from the app for review or support.
screen capture.context: Screenshots page, screen recording controls, capture deletion, and save-status messages.
screen capture.translationRule: Translate as screen capture/screenshot/recording based on source string. Do not imply camera photos.
screen capture.meaning: A screenshot or screen recording produced by Pinokio, stored locally for the user.

prototype.purpose: Name the guided app/launcher creation wizard for drafting a new Pinokio project.
prototype.context: Prototype wizard route, project setup steps, AI prompt collection, and starter type choices.
prototype.translationRule: Translate as prototype/draft builder. Preserve that it creates app/launcher scaffolding, not a production deployment promise.
prototype.meaning: A wizard for generating or importing a starter Pinokio project/launcher.

example placeholder.purpose: Show the expected shape of command, path, URL, or repository inputs without changing executable content.
example placeholder.context: Prototype wizard placeholders, init/import forms, task repository placeholders, and command examples.
example placeholder.translationRule: Translate words such as example/e.g. only. Preserve commands, paths, package names, URLs, and repository slugs exactly unless they are intentionally locale-specific sample prose.
example placeholder.meaning: Input hint text containing literal examples the user may adapt.

file changed on disk.purpose: Warn that a file opened in Pinokio was modified outside the current editor session.
file changed on disk.context: Files app status messages and reload-from-disk confirmation.
file changed on disk.translationRule: Translate as an external disk/filesystem change. Do not imply the user edited the current unsaved buffer.
file changed on disk.meaning: The saved file on disk differs from the editor session metadata and may need reload or conflict handling.
